pi_items.source: Task name is specified consistently across task.json:17, task description (line 5), setup_task.sh:17 (DELETE query), export_result.sh:23 (SELECT query), and verifier.py:35 (default value). The task name is a custom name for this scheduler configuration task.

Class name is specified in task.json:18, task description (line 5), and verifier.py:36 (default value). Verified as a real OpenMRS scheduler task class from: (1) OpenMRS API documentation (docs.openmrs.org) listing AlertReminderTask as a direct subclass of AbstractTask; (2) GitHub openmrs-core repository confirming AlertReminderTask exists in org.openmrs.scheduler.tasks package; (3) OpenMRS docs confirming AlertReminderTask is a sample implementation for sending emails/alerts via message service.

Repeat interval (in seconds) is specified in task.json:19, task description mentions '3600 seconds', and verifier.py:37 checks for this exact value. 3600 seconds = 3600/3600 = 1 hour, consistent with the task name 'Hourly Alert Sync'. Verified as a standard column in OpenMRS scheduler_task_config table (confirmed via OpenMRS documentation and database schema references).

Boolean flag specified in task.json:20, task description mentions 'Start on Startup: Yes'. Value 1 represents boolean true (task should start when scheduler starts). Verified as a standard column in OpenMRS scheduler_task_config table. Verifier.py:101-106 checks that this value is 1, 'true', or 'True'.

Boolean flag specified in task.json:21. Value 1 represents boolean true (task should be actively running). Verified as the 'started' column in OpenMRS scheduler_task_config table. Verifier.py:109-114 checks that this value is 1, 'true', or 'True' to confirm the task is running.

privileged_info_summary: The create_scheduler_task requires configuring an OpenMRS background scheduler task named 'Hourly Alert Sync' with the schedulable class 'org.openmrs.scheduler.tasks.AlertReminderTask'. The task is configured to run every 3600 seconds (1 hour), start automatically on scheduler startup, and be in a running (started) state. All metadata values are verified: AlertReminderTask is a documented, real OpenMRS scheduler class; 3600 seconds equals one hour, consistent with the task's name and purpose; the start_on_startup and started values are standard OpenMRS scheduler task configuration flags. The task uses synthetic test data - it creates a configuration entry in the local Bahmni/OpenMRS system, not based on any external or real data source.
